options: Deep and durable control over a political system
questions.exp: STRUCTURAL DOMINANCE = deep, long-term control over a system. Example usage from the editorial: the election results signal the BJP’s structural political dominance.
JUGGERNAUT = a powerful force that is difficult to stop. Example usage from the editorial: the BJP-dominant system is described as a juggernaut.

options: Calling or positioning people into a social identity
questions.exp: INTERPELLATIVE = shaping people into a particular identity or role. Example usage from the editorial: government procedures have interpellative capacity in Assam.

options: Resistance to dominant power or ideology
questions.exp: COUNTER-HEGEMONY = resistance to a dominant ideology or power structure. Example usage from the editorial: withstanding the BJP-dominant system requires counter-hegemony.

options: Removal or denial of voting rights
questions.exp: DISENFRANCHISEMENT = denial or removal of voting rights. Example usage from the editorial: SIR controversies raised questions of disenfranchisement.
REPUDIATION = strong rejection or refusal. Example usage from the editorial: the Bengal result was described as public repudiation of a political culture.
ADJUDICATION = the process of making a formal legal judgment. Example usage from the editorial: voter numbers under adjudication may affect the electoral outcome.

options: Strongly biased towards a party or cause
questions.exp: PARTISAN = biased in favour of a party or side. Example usage from the editorial: the PM’s broadcast raised questions about partisan use of public media.
COERCIVE = involving force or pressure. Example usage from the editorial: courts restrained authorities from coercive action against forest dwellers.

options: Flexible and capable of wider interpretation
questions.exp: OPEN-TEXTURED = flexible and not rigidly limited. Example usage from the editorial: the MCC is described as open-textured.
DURABLE = long-lasting or stable. Its antonym is TEMPORARY. Example usage from the editorial: structural dominance is described as durable.
COHERENT = logically connected and consistent. Its antonym is FRAGMENTED. Example usage from the editorial: scattered grievances were turned into a coherent narrative.
PRECARITY = insecurity or unstable condition. Its antonym is SECURITY. Example usage from the editorial: young voters face precarity and uncertainty.
TENDENTIOUSNESS = biased or agenda-driven expression. Its antonym is NEUTRALITY. Example usage from the editorial: spectacle prevailed over old-style political tendentiousness.

questions.q: Idiom: **'bark up the wrong tree'** means:
options: Choose the wrong course of action
questions.exp: BARK UP THE WRONG TREE = to follow the wrong course or blame the wrong person. Example usage from the newspaper’s language section: the idiom means making the wrong choice.
SETTLE THAT QUESTION = resolve or decide an issue. Example usage from the editorial: the election results should settle the question of structural dominance.
STRUCK OFF = removed from a list or record. Example usage from the editorial: a support base was struck off from the electoral list.
BROUGHT IN = introduced or included. Example usage from the editorial: awareness drives have brought more people into the electoral process.
DEPOLITICISES = removes political character or active political engagement. Example usage from the editorial: managerial politics depoliticises the electorate.
STATUTORY = created, required, or regulated by law. Example usage from the editorial: the Gram Sabha is described as a statutory authority under the Forest Rights Act.
